reviews.review_description: I was a big fan of ball smash hit on the oculus go and the one for the Meta quest three is a big disappointment! It’s almost as if they did it to cheat the audience out of money and knowing ￼￼ purposely, that the game isn’t ￼ good. ￼ ￼￼

I was a big fan of the original Smash Hit and this is one is still miles away, BUT it was recently released so I'm hoping they'll be improving the graphics (the original game's looked better even on the Oculus Go), adding levels (there is only one!) and also speed! I would definetly remove the guns.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your genuine and honest feedback 🙏
I completely understand your point, and I feel the same way — I am also a big fan of the original Smash Hit.

This game has been released recently, and i'm new in vr development, so I’m still learning and improving step by step. I am actively working on better graphics, improved gameplay feel, more levels, and overall speed. Your feedback about the guns is also noted, and I’ll seriously consider it for future updates.

My goal is definitely to move closer to the original Smash Hit–style experience, and I will keep updating the game until the expected graphics and gameplay quality are achieved.

Thank you again for taking the time to share your thoughts — feedback like this really helps me improve the game 🙌

reviews.review_description: Had a Gear VR in the past and this game was simple but fun. Now it sucks. Aiming is so much worse now compared to when I didn't even have a controller. Breaking barriers would allow you to pass even if the physics engine glitched and didn't drop all the pieces now it counts as a loss. Massive downgrade imo. Give me the old Smash Hit back.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your experience — I genuinely respect your opinion.

I understand your frustration, especially if you enjoyed the older version and the simplicity it had on Gear VR. The aiming and physics changes clearly haven’t worked well for you, and that’s valuable feedback for me. The issue you mentioned about barriers and physics glitches causing a loss is something I take seriously and will look into improving.

This is a newer version and a fresh VR build, and I’m still working to refine the controls, physics behavior, and overall game feel. My intention was never to downgrade the experience, but to rebuild and improve it step by step.

I truly appreciate you taking the time to point out what didn’t work for you. Feedback like this helps guide future updates, and I’ll keep working to make the gameplay smoother and more enjoyable again.

Thank you for being honest.
